name: Librilla, Alhama de Murcia y Pliego por el Collado de Espuña — return from Alcantarilla
name_original: Librilla, Alhama de Murcia y Pliego por el Collado de Espuña — vuelta desde Alcantarilla
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Librilla are situated in the Guadalentín Valley, a pre-coastal depression divided by the Rambla de Librilla. The region features diverse landscapes, including citrus orchards, almond groves, olive trees, vineyards, and protected pinewoods. Surrounding mountain ranges such as Sierra de la Muela, Sierra de Carrascoy, and Sierra Espuña provide varied terrain for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Librilla The most popular road cycling route is Librilla, Alhama de Murcia y Pliego por el Collado de Espuña — return from Alcantarilla, a 38.0 miles (61.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through multiple towns and varied agricultural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Collado Mangueta Viewpoint – Collado Bermejo loop from Librilla, a difficult 42.5 miles (68.4 km) path. This route includes a significant ascent to Collado Bermejo within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, offering challenging climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Castle of Alhama de Murcia – Avenida de las Fuerzas Armadas loop from Librilla, a 31.5 miles (50.7 km) trail leading through historical areas and open countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Librilla is defined by the Guadalentín Valley, the Sierra Espuña mountains, and extensive agricultural lands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Librilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Librilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Librilla?
A: There are 11 road cycling routes around Librilla, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Librilla?
A: The routes around Librilla cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 7 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, including challenging ascents in the Sierra Espuña Regional Park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling near Librilla?
A: Road cycling routes in Librilla traverse diverse landscapes within the Guadalentín Valley. You'll pass through citrus orchards, almond groves, olive trees, vineyards, and protected pinewoods. The routes also offer views of surrounding mountain ranges like Sierra de la Muela, Sierra de Carrascoy, and Sierra Espuña.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, the region offers significant challenges for experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Mangueta Viewpoint – Collado Bermejo loop from Librilla&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes a substantial ascent to Collado Bermejo, a prominent Category 1 climb within the Sierra Espuña National Park known for its length and numerous hairpins.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced riders?
A: While many routes offer moderate challenges, there is at least one easy route available. The region's Greenways also provide dedicated cycling paths, which can be a good option for less experienced riders looking for smoother, less trafficked surfaces, though specific easy road cycling routes are limited.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Librilla?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Mangueta Viewpoint – Collado Bermejo loop from Librilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40387217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alhama de Murcia – Avenida de las Fuerzas Armadas loop from Librilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I visit on a road cycling route near Librilla?
A: Several routes offer access to stunning viewpoints. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4057769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algeciras Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6168855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, both providing panoramic vistas of the Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir and its mountainous surroundings.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I explore while cycling in the region?
A: Beyond the scenic views, you can encounter unique natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the surreal badlands of Barrancos de Gebas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt; is another notable summit in the area.

Q: Are there any historical sites along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, routes often pass through or near old villages with Spanish and Moorish architecture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40387217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alhama de Murcia – Avenida de las Fuerzas Armadas loop from Librilla&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through historical areas, offering glimpses into the region's rich past.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Librilla?
A: The road cycling routes in Librilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the well-paved, quiet roads and the diverse scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Librilla?
A: The region of Murcia generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures ideal for longer rides and challenging climbs. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there routes that pass through the Sierra Espuña Regional Park?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra Espuña Regional Park is a major draw for cyclists, known for its smooth roads, pine forests, and significant climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Mangueta Viewpoint – Collado Bermejo loop from Librilla&lt;/a&gt; take you directly into this expansive protected area, offering challenging ascents and stunning views.
